summary: Parallel review: FAIL (5 critical, 11 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1617` — `activePhases2` hardcodes `["brainstorm", "build", "review"]`; `"simplify"` absent despite `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` at run.mjs:1506. Simplify-phase token costs won't appear in the progress.md "By phase" breakdown, even though the console output at line 1591 correctly includes it. Silent cost reporting gap on every run that hits the simplify pass.

The feature is architecturally sound. `runSimplifyFixLoop` is correctly extracted to `simplify-pass.mjs` with a clean data boundary, the escalation guard at run.mjs:1538 unconditionally protects `harness("finalize")`, the loop is bounded to 3 dispatches, and all 67 tests pass. The one new 🟡 (missing `"simplify"` in `activePhases2`) should go to backlog. No 🔴 findings.
All five prior 🟡 findings from the previous review cycle were addressed:

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1617 — `activePhases2` still hardcodes `["brainstorm", "build", "review"]`; task-3 fixed `phaseOrder` (line 1591, console) but not this variable (progress.md "By phase" summary); simplify token costs appear in CLI output but are silently dropped from the written `progress.md` artifact — add `"simplify"` to complete the fix

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1508 — When a fix round returns `reverted: true` with `findings.critical > 0`, the next iteration dispatches the agent on identical code (changes already undone); all remaining rounds deterministically revert and escalate with no path to progress — bounded and safe, but wastes agent calls; carried from prior PM review
The core requirement — critical simplicity findings block `finalize` with a 2-round fix loop and escalation — is fully implemented and correctly tested. Two 🟡 items go to backlog. No blockers.
- `run.mjs` lines 1497–1620 — `activePhases2` at line 1617 confirmed to omit `"simplify"` (real carried 🟡); all other claims verified

**Prior 🟡 gaps resolved by task-3:** behavioral loop tests (now direct behavioral), dispatch-fail missing findings (now fixed and asserted), escalated absent from completion summary (now present at run.mjs:1580). No 🔴 or 🟡 findings remain from the test-coverage lens.

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:82` — File paths from `git diff --name-only` embedded verbatim in LLM prompt; fix loop re-dispatches up to 3× with `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`; strip control characters before embedding: `f.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "").trim()` — carried from prior security passes, **amplified by fix loop**

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:47` — `getChangedFiles` accepts caller-supplied `base` with no SHA format validation before `execSync` shell interpolation; add `/^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$/i` guard — carried from prior security passes
**No critical (🔴) findings.** The security story is straightforward for this local developer tool:

The two 🟡 warnings (unsanitized filenames in LLM prompt, unvalidated SHA in public API) are both carried from prior review cycles — task-3 amplifies the first one by replaying the unsanitized brief up to 3× each with `bypassPermissions`. Neither blocks merge. Detailed evaluation written to `.team/features/self-simplification-pass/tasks/task-3/eval.md`.

🟡 bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:233 — `runPassFn(round)` passes `round` to the callback, but the only caller `() => runSimplifyPass({...})` ignores it; the round value is already used for logging (line 231) — passing it to the callback is speculative extensibility with no stated requirement; change to `runPassFn()` or document the intent
No 🔴 critical findings. The four veto categories:
- **Gold-plating** — WARNING (🟡). `runPassFn(round)` passes a loop index to a callback that no caller uses — speculative extensibility with no stated requirement. Borderline; doesn't block merge.
